title: 10 best herbal essential oils for bladder infection
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for bladder infection are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the symptoms and discomfort associated with urinary tract infections. These oils contain antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in the bladder. Some of the best herbal essential oils for bladder infection include Juniperus communis, Equisetum arvense, Cinchona officinalis, Berberis vulgaris, Sambucus nigra, Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, Astragalus membranaceus, Solanum dulcamara, Melissa officinalis, and Echinacea purpurea. If left untreated, bladder infections can lead to severe consequences, including chronic kidney damage, urinary retention, and even sepsis, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry essential oils helps with bladder infection because it possesses antibacterial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause urinary tract infections. Its main component, arbutin, is converted into hydroquinone in the body, which has been shown to reduce bacterial adhesion and prevent bacterial biofilms from forming on the bladder wall. This helps to alleviate symptoms such as burning during urination, frequent urination, and pelvic pain, promoting a healthy urinary tract and reducing the risk of recurrence.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Bearberry essential oil has antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of bladder infection.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of bearberry essential oil help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by bladder infection, providing relief from pain and urgency to urinate.

Antiseptic: Bearberry essential oil has antiseptic properties that prevent bacterial growth and promote healing in the urinary tract, reducing the risk of complications and promoting overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bearberry plant contain a high concentration of uva-ursi, which has been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and inflammation.

Barks: The barks of the bearberry plant contain compounds like arbutin and hydroquinone that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective for treating bladder infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems and 4 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba.
Combine the dried herb mixture with the carrier oil in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks.
Shake the jar daily for 15 minutes to ensure thorough mixing and allow the herb's natural compounds to infuse into the oil.
After 2 weeks, strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Store the herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid and use within 6 months for optimal potency and aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's mucilaginous consistency coats and protects the bladder lining, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by bacterial invasion. Additionally, marshmallow oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause infections, promoting a healthy balance in the urinary tract. By using marshmallow essential oils, individuals can experience relief from symptoms such as burning sensations during urination and frequent urges to empty the bladder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with bladder infections.
Soothing and protective property: The mucilages present in marshmallow root create a protective barrier on the bladder lining, soothing irritation and reducing inflammation.
Antioxidant property: Marshmallow root contains antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals and protect the urinary tract from damage caused by infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections, including bladder infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Buds: The buds of the marshmallow plant contain mucilages that help soothe and calm irritated bladders, reducing inflammation and pain associated with bladder infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried marshmallow root powder from althaea officinalis plant.
Steam distill the dried powder using 10 grams per 100 milliliters of water for 3 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the aqueous fraction through decantation.
Combine 15 drops of the extracted essential oil with 5 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Store the final blend in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its potent antibacterial properties that effectively combat bacterial overgrowth in the urinary tract. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with bladder infections, while its antispasmodic properties ease painful urination and frequency. Additionally, highbush cranberry's astringent properties may help shrink and heal inflamed tissue, promoting overall urinary health and reducing the risk of recurring infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Highbush cranberry essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, helping to combat bacterial overgrowth in the urinary tract that can cause bladder infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with bladder infections, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms such as burning during urination.

Antioxidant activity: Highbush cranberry essential oil's high antioxidant content helps protect the urinary tract lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bladder infection development.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of highbush cranberry are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against bacterial infections and inflammation associated with bladder infections.
Rhyzomes: Highbush cranberry rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and other genitourinary issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and stems at dawn for maximum potency.
Cleanse the plant material by washing it gently in cold running water followed by distilled water rinse.
Combine 200 grams of clean plant material with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil in a glass container.
Steam distill the mixture for exactly four hours at a temperature range of 100 to 110 degrees celsius under vacuum pressure.
Strain and filter the resulting essential oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter paper and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), including E. coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae. Additionally, goldenrod essential oils' anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce discomfort and inflammation associated with bladder infection symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and relief from symptoms such as burning during urination and frequent urination.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Goldenrod contains compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including those that can cause UTIs.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod has anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin that may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate symptoms of UTIs.
Uric acid reduction: Goldenrod has been traditionally used to help reduce uric acid levels in the body, which can contribute to kidney stones and other urinary issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Goldenrod contain bioactive compounds that help soothe inflammation in the urinary tract, making them effective against bladder infections.
Seeds: Goldenrod seeds have been traditionally used to treat kidney and bladder issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers in the morning.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or low-temperature drying methods within 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried solidago virgaurea with 500ml of distilled water for 3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and store at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before using in a dilution ratio of 2% with carrier oils.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil contains compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and kill bacteria that can cause infections. Additionally, dandelion oil has been traditionally used to support kidney and bladder health by increasing urine production and reducing the risk of bacterial growth. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate symptoms of bladder infection and promote overall urinary tract wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Dandelion extracts contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which may alleviate symptoms of bladder infections.

Diuretic and antiseptic properties: Dandelion root and leaves have been traditionally used to stimulate urine production and help flush out bacteria from the urinary tract, while also possessing antimicrobial properties that can combat bacterial growth.

Anti-mutagenic and anti-carcinogenic properties: Some studies suggest that dandelion extracts may possess anti-cancer properties and be effective against certain types of cancer cells, including those associated with bladder cancer.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and alleviating pain associated with bladder infections.
Seeds: Containing bioactive compounds like fatty acids and terpenes, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat urinary tract infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in small bunches using paper bags or desiccant packs for 7-10 days.
Combine dried dandelion flowers with carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Infuse the mixture in a warm dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily and monitoring temperature at 60-70 degrees fahrenheit.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate oil and solids, then store in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's compounds, such as scopoletin and silibinin, work to combat harmful bacteria that cause UTIs, while also reducing inflammation in the urinary tract. Additionally, stinging nettle's diuretic properties help to increase urine flow, flushing out bacteria and toxins from the body, providing relief from symptoms like burning sensation during urination and frequent trips to the bathroom.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with urinary tract infections (UTIs).
Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial compounds in stinging nettle essential oil have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, such as E. coli, which are common causes of bladder infections.
Urolithinase inhibiting property: Stinging nettle essential oil has been found to inhibit the formation of kidney stones and gravel, which can contribute to UTIs.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of bladder infection.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antimicrobial properties, inhibiting bacterial growth and helping to combat bladder infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried nettle leaves measuring 10 grams for every 100 milliliters of carrier oil.
Combine the dried nettle leaves with the carrier oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container to separate the herbal infusion from solids.
Store the herbal infusion in an amber glass bottle and let it sit at room temperature for 7 days allowing the essential oils to mature.
Filter the resulting liquid again using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids before transferring to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the urinary tract. The oil's antibacterial compounds also target the bacteria that cause UTIs, helping to eliminate the infection. Additionally, ginger has been shown to improve blood flow and increase urine production, which can aid in flushing out the bacteria and debris from the bladder and urinary tract, promoting a swift recovery from an infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger oil has potent anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with bladder infections, making it easier to urinate and relieving discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: Ginger oil contains antimicrobial agents that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for urinary tract infections (UTIs), such as E. coli, helping to clear up the infection and prevent further complications.

Antioxidant property: Herbal ginger oil's antioxidant properties help protect the bladder lining from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healthy bladder function and reducing the risk of future UTI recurrences.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the ginger plant are rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe bladder irritation and reduce inflammation associated with bladder infections.

Roots: Ginger roots contain a high concentration of essential oils and have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and other genitourinary issues due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes of zingiber officinale weighing approximately 500 grams.
Wash the harvested ginger thoroughly with distilled water removing any dirt or debris.
Slice the cleaned ginger into thin pieces and dry them using a food dehydrator at 65°c for 6 hours.
Steam distill the dried ginger slices using a clevenger apparatus for 3 hours collecting approximately 50 milliliters of essential oil.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities or residue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its natural antibacterial properties, which help combat E. coli bacteria that typically cause UTIs. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried berberis aquifolium leaves and stems.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried plant material.
Collect the distillate in a separate container and allow it to settle for 30-60 minutes.
Decant and filter the clear essential oil using a paper coffee filter or a glass fiber filter.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as chamazulene and thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), including E. coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a copper alembic still with 10 liters of distilled water.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the water using a separator funnel with 5% of the collected volume.
Bottle the extracted essential oil in dark glass vials and store in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The oil's active compounds, such as calendulin and triterpenoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause urinary tract infections (UTIs), including E. coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing discomfort associated with bladder infections.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have antimicrobial and antiseptic effects, making them effective in combating bacterial infections in the bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom ensuring optimal potency for the oil extraction process.
Dry harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low setting for 8 hours to preserve quality.
Use a high-quality essential oil extractor like a crock-pot or double boiler extracting the oil from dried flowers for 6 hours.
Strain and filter extracted oil through cheesecloth or coffee filters removing impurities and sedimentation particles ensuring clarity.
Allow the filtered oil to cool and settle before bottling and storing in dark glass containers at room temperature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bladder infection with herbal essential oils is to dilute Uva Ursi oil in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it topically to the lower abdomen. Combine this with regular bathroom trips and increased water intake to help flush out bacteria. Additionally, add a few drops of Tea Tree oil to your bath water for its antimicrobial properties. This holistic approach can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing from within.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bladder infection that are treated with herbal essential oils are urinary tract infections (UTIs), kidney stones, and prostatitis. Essential oils such as uva ursi, juniper berry, and sandalwood are commonly used to treat these conditions due to their antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. These oils can help relieve symptoms like burning during urination, frequent urination, and pelvic pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus communis essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's constituents, such as alpha-pinene and limonene, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause infections. Juniperus communis also helps to dissolve kidney stones and improve urine flow, which can aid in the elimination of bacteria from the urinary tract. Additionally, its diuretic properties help to increase urine production, further flushing out the infection. This makes Juniperus communis essential oil a popular natural remedy for urinary tract infections.
remedy_constituents: Terpinen-4-ol: It acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to combat bacterial infections that cause bladder infections.
Cadinene: It has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bladder infections.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, helping to eliminate bacteria and other pathogens that cause bladder infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain juniperine, a compound that has diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and relieve symptoms of bladder infections.

Fruits: Fruits are used because they contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, including terpenes and flavonoids, that can help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature juniperus communis berries in late summer when they have turned a deep blue-purple color.
Dry the berries in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill the dried berries using a still with a 10:1 water-to-herb ratio for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled water and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and debris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause urinary tract infections. Additionally, Equisetum arvense oil has been traditionally used to treat conditions related to the urinary system, including kidney stones and incontinence. Its ability to soothe and calm the urinary tract also helps to reduce discomfort and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Equisetum flavone glycosides: These flavone glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections in the bladder and alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Furanosesquiterpenoids: Furanosesquiterpenoids possess antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for bladder infections, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing.

Furanosesquiterpene lactones: Furanosesquiterpene lactones exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe the bladder and reduce inflammation, making it easier to recover from bladder infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate bladder infection symptoms.
Stems: Used due to their ability to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.
Leaves: Used due to their antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections in the bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense stems at dawn or late afternoon to minimize damage and maximize oil content.
Steam distill 100 grams of fresh equisetum arvense stems for 3-4 hours at a pressure of 2-3 bar.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the equisetum arvense essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature below 20 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with bladder infection because its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties effectively combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. The oil's quinine content has natural antimicrobial properties that target and eliminate the root cause of the infection. Additionally, its ability to balance pH levels and soothe the bladder muscles helps to alleviate discomfort and promote a speedy recovery. remedy_constituents: Quinovic acid glycosides: Help with bladder infection by exhibiting antibacterial properties, which can reduce bacterial load and alleviate symptoms.
Quinic acid: Contribute to the treatment of bladder infection by having antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe and protect the bladder lining.
Cinchonine: Assist in treating bladder infection by demonstrating antiseptic properties, which can inhibit the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that cause the infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of quinine and other alkaloids that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat bacterial infections in the bladder.
Barks: Barks are rich in quinoline alkaloids, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective in treating urinary tract infections and reducing inflammation.

Roots: Roots of Cinchona officinalis contain alkaloids that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with bladder infections, such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinchona officinalis leaves and stems during early morning hours when dew is present.
Steam distill 1 kg of cinchona officinalis leaves and stems in a 10-liter distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation or centrifugation.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any remaining impurities or particulate matter.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at a temperature of 10-20°c for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris essential oils helps with bladder infection because it has potent antimicrobial properties that target E. coli bacteria, a common cause of UTIs. Buds: Buds: The unopened flowers, which have antibacterial properties and contain alkaloids that help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of berberis vulgaris in the morning after the dew has evaporated with 2 kg of roots.
Steam distill the harvested roots at a temperature of 100°c to 110°c for 3 to 4 hours.
Collect the obtained distillate and separate it from the water using a separation funnel for 30 minutes.
Transfer the collected essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place for 24 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper to remove any impurities and obtain the final product.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): They reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary tract infections, including pain and discomfort.

remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to help combat bladder infections by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves at full bloom to ensure high oil content.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100°c for 3 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 5°c.
Analyze the final essential oil using gc-ms to ensure purity and quality standards are met.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arctostaphylos uva-ursi essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 2 pounds of fresh arctostaphylos uva-ursi leaves and berries in the early morning.
Dry the collected plant material in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material for 2 hours at 2 bars to obtain the essential oil.
Collect and filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and excess plant material.
Store the obtained arctostaphylos uva-ursi essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 15 degrees celsius.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of astragalus membranaceus in the early fall season when the plant is mature and fully grown.

Steam distill the dried roots for 3-4 hours using a distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water using a separator funnel.

Its bioactive compounds, such as solasodine and solanine, have been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the bladder and surrounding tissues. By addressing the root cause of the infection, Solanum dulcamara essential oils can provide relief and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of bladder infection by inhibiting the growth of bacteria.

Phenolic acids (e.g., rosmarinic acid): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat bacterial infections in the bladder and alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol): These compounds have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of bladder infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections associated with bladder issues.
Stems: The stems of Solanum dulcamara are used in essential oil production to leverage their antiseptic properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.
Flowers: Solanum dulcamara flowers are used to create essential oils that possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of bladder infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of solanum dulcamara flowers and leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Steam distill the plant material in a still at a temperature range of 40-60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect 50ml of essential oil that has a yield of 1.5% from the distillation process after 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a 100-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and particles.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with bladder infection because it possesses antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial growth, reducing the severity of symptoms. Its antispasmodic properties also help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with bladder infections. Additionally, Melissa officinalis oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the urinary tract, promoting a healthy environment for healing. Its natural properties work synergistically to address the underlying causes of bladder infections, providing relief and promoting recovery.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: A terpene that helps to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, making it easier to combat bladder infections.
Linalool: A terpene that has antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate the bacteria that cause bladder infections.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and protect the bladder and urinary tract from infection and damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly limonene, which has antimicrobial properties effective against urinary tract infections.

Stems: Stems are also used because they contain a high amount of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can help alleviate bladder infection symptoms.

Buds: Buds are another commonly used part, as they contain a high concentration of essential oils, including geraniol and nerol, which have antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that can help combat bladder infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the melissa leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal oil production.
Steam distill the harvested melissa leaves at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to separate oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the final melissa essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with bladder infection because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including E. coli, which is a common cause of urinary tract infections. Additionally, Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with bladder infections, such as pain and discomfort. This natural remedy can provide relief and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Alkalamines: These alkaloids have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections such as those causing bladder infections.
Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with bladder infections.
Polyacetylenes: These terpenes have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat bladder infections.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections in the bladder.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the bladder from infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried echinacea purpurea flowers from mid to late summer when they are fully open.
Steam distill the flowers in a 1:10 ratio of plant material to distilled water for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator or decanting.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and contaminants immediately.
Store the echinacea purpurea essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat.
